source_notes: Brunei LNG Sdn Bhd plant at Lumut commissioned 1972. Original 20-yr SPA signed 1969 with Mitsubishi/Tokyo Gas/Tokyo Electric for the full 5.14 Mtpa nameplate. World's first major LNG export project to Japan. Source: Shell Brunei LNG history; JOGMEC Asia-Pacific LNG chronology.

Arun LNG (North Sumatra) and Bontang LNG (East Kalimantan) signed 1973 SPAs with Japanese utilities consortium. First cargo from Bontang to Senboku terminal Aug 1977. Pertamina-operated. Initial contracted ~7.5 Mtpa across both plants, ramping to >25 Mtpa by mid-1980s. Source: Pertamina LNG history; JOGMEC.

ADGAS Das Island LNG plant, Abu Dhabi. First cargo to Tokyo Electric April 1977. Original 20-yr SPA: TEPCO 2.3 Mtpa. Plant operated by ADNOC/Mitsui/BP/Total JV. Source: ADGAS company history; Oil & Gas Journal historical records.

MLNG Satu (Bintulu, Sarawak) commissioned Jan 1983. 20-yr SPAs with Tokyo Electric, Tokyo Gas, Saibu Gas totaling 6.0 Mtpa from inception. Petronas-operated. Source: Petronas LNG history; Wood Mackenzie.

North West Shelf Trains 1-2 commissioned 1989. 1985 SPAs with 8-utility Japanese consortium (TEPCO, Tokyo Gas, Chubu, Kansai, Osaka Gas, Kyushu, Toho, Nippon Steel) for 5.0 Mtpa. First cargo Karratha to Sodegaura 28 Jul 1989. Source: data/manual/lng_contracts_aus_jpn.csv; Lexology 50-yr LNG history.

Qatargas 1 commissioned 1996; first cargo to Japan early 1997. 25-yr SPAs with Chubu Electric, Tohoku, Kansai, Tokyo Electric, Kyushu, Toho, Osaka Gas totaling ~4 Mtpa initially (expanded later with RasGas trains). Source: Qatargas company history.
Oman LNG plant at Qalhat commissioned 2000. 25-yr SPAs with Osaka Gas, Kansai Electric, Tokyo Electric totaling ~4.1 Mtpa. Source: Oman LNG; JOGMEC.
